media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots

characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: See also his entryhereas many of the same tropes still apply here. Big Brother Mentor: He's taken up this role toward the children of San Lorenzo, often to the ire of Senora Zapata. Character Development: He slowly grows throughout the show, to the point where when he exiles himself from San Lorenzo, he tries to assert to himself that he is the same loner rogue he was before, but has to admit he isn't. Disney Death: During the last episode, he has to fight against the third Tulpa. However,Dulcinea finds out that she is the White Tulpa, who can use a magic sword that can fire magic beams, and this causes her to temporarily lose her morality and fight against Puss.She only snaps out of it after her magic beam hit him. Since the beams from the White Tulpa's sword kill anyone they hit, Puss dies, but Dulcinea admits her love for him and gives him a kiss on the cheek, which revives him. Heroic Sacrifice: Hegoes back in time and ensures he never enters San Lorenzo, and consequently, never meets any of the people he has come to love, in order to protect the world, though he retains his memories of them.

characters.name: Puss in Boots
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: See also his entryhereas many of the same tropes still apply here. Big Brother Mentor: He's taken up this role toward the children of San Lorenzo, often to the ire of Senora Zapata. Character Development: He slowly grows throughout the show, to the point where when he exiles himself from San Lorenzo, he tries to assert to himself that he is the same loner rogue he was before, but has to admit he isn't. Disney Death: During the last episode, he has to fight against the third Tulpa. However,Dulcinea finds out that she is the White Tulpa, who can use a magic sword that can fire magic beams, and this causes her to temporarily lose her morality and fight against Puss.She only snaps out of it after her magic beam hit him. Since the beams from the White Tulpa's sword kill anyone they hit, Puss dies, but Dulcinea admits her love for him and gives him a kiss on the cheek, which revives him. Heroic Sacrifice: Hegoes back in time and ensures he never enters San Lorenzo, and consequently, never meets any of the people he has come to love, in order to protect the world, though he retains his memories of them.

characters.name: Dulcinea
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: A sweet and innocent female white cat whom Puss falls for and follows to the town of San Lorenzo, starting off the plot. Throughout her adventures with Puss in Boots, she develops into a more confident and capable person, which culminates in her ultimately becomingThe Chosen Onethat defeats the Bloodwolf. Four-Fingered Hands: The fact that she has these when others don't is important. Ambiguous Situation:While she makes it past theGrand Finale, it's hard to say if she and Puss are still together, or if she's even still alive by the time ofShrek 2or even Puss' solo films. As the Good Book Says...: Often quotes passages from herWee Compendium of Factes and Funneto make a point, having based her entire life on its teachings.Shedoesn't take wellto learning its author considers it gibberish, but manages to stay devoted to the spirit of the book. Beware the Nice Ones: She has shown a willingness to kill to protect her town from evil. See the spoiler text inThou Shalt Not Killfor details. And she can get outright devious where Jack Sprat is concerned, though he definitely deserves it.

characters.name: Artephius
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: A senile old man who was once a great alchemist, but these days prefers to run a macrame shop which he named Owlberto. Ambiguous Situation: It's a little unclear just how much he'sObfuscating Stupidity, partuclarly when it comes toThe Reveal, which he does allude to a few times. Casting Gag. It isn'tthe first timePaul Rugg has voiceda bearded, eccentric old wizard. Cloudcuckoolander: His insane ramblings (that nevertheless often turn out to be true in some way) and lack of coherence are aRunning Gag. Forgetful Jones: He can forget things mere seconds after they happen.

characters.name: Mayor Temoroso
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: The cowardly mayor of San Lorenzo. Despite his large size, he would very much prefer to be hidden and out of sight, spending most of his time hiding inside barrels. Hidden Depths: He likes and knows all about bees, including how to communicate with them. He also makes all the barrels he hides in himself. Large and in Charge: Despite being a complete coward, he is quite large and in charge of the town.Puss: (Upon finally seeing the mayor out of the small barrel) MR. MAYOR!!! YOU'RE HUGE!!! Lovable Coward: To compensate, he's learned how to make his barrels, and how to use them in combat. Meek Townsman: The mayor's reaction to any situation that might be considered even slightly threatening is to hide in a barrel.

characters.name: Pajuna
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: A cow that runs the local Cow and Moone Cantina. Constantly Lactating Cow: She is an anthropomorphic cow who serves her own milk. She doesn't have any calves, and there is no sign of her ever being pregnant over the entire course of the series. Furry Reminder: It is constantly made clear that it is her own milk she is serving. Special blends are all created by her eating particular things, or in the case of milkshakes, putting her udder on ice before she milks herself. Mr. Vice Guy: Kind and down-to-earth as she is, she's averyopportunistic businesswoman. Though she lets Puss have a limitless tab, she takes every chance to reel in customers that she can - which is why the prison is in her cantina. And when she finds out the San Lorenzans (and Puss) have never heard of games of chance, she wastes no time setting up anChuck E. Cheese-esque system to fleece everyone of their cash, and while she feels bad about it when it nearly ruins the town she makes no effort to stop. Mysterious Past: She starts dropping hints of her past in Season 2, all taking the form of aNoodle Incident. For example, when evacuating San Lorenzo is considered, she says she can't leave because things of hers are buried under the town, and that these objectsare not proof of crimes she committed.

characters.name: Senora Zapata
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: The owner of the San Lorenzo orphanage. Extremely distrustful of outsiders, she holds a lasting grudge against Puss in Boots. Fantastic Racism: Hates goblins because one of them stood her up on a date. Insistent Terminology: She consistently refers to Puss as "Puss in the Boots", and rarely, if ever shortening it, unlike everyone else. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: For all her flaws, she really cares about the orphans, even making it a point that they shouldn't be spoiled too much. Not So Above It All: She warms up to thebabydragon while it's in town, a kid's doll stolen by thieves in an episode belongs to her and Esme claims to have seen her kiss a thief in "Stories".

characters.name: Esme
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: Beware the Nice Ones: She is dangerous with that teddy bear when she wants to be. Children Are Innocent: Generally the most cute and naive of the orphans. That said.. Troubling Unchildlike Behavior: During the year span when Puss and Dulcinea were stuck in the Netherworld after Season 5, Esme becomesa mob boss.A feared one, no less. All without changing her cutesy personality one bit.

characters.name: Kid Pickles
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: A pickle-loving orphan living in San Lorenzo. Birthday Hater: They generally don't involve pickles enough to interest him. Brooklyn Rage: Inexplicably has a "noir tough guy" accent. Definitely has the spunk to match. Improbable Weapon User: Whenever he has to fight, he usually throws pickles. Or smacks people with pickles. Or stuffs pickles into people's noses. And so on. Mouthy Kid: Has the most moxie and snark of any of the orphans, and is most likely to snap back at the bad guys.

characters.name: Vina
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: Cool Big Sis: Sometimes plays this role for most of the other kids being the oldest and most skilled at games. Fantastic Racism: Shows this towards Cleevil at first, albeit only because Senora Zapata convinced her of it. In truth she was jealous, and once she got over that, she had little problem being friends. Green-Eyed Monster: To Cleevil when she first appeared, untilher jealousy accidentally led to the townspeople rejecting Cleevil entirely. Kick Chick: Her specialty after Puss trains her to defend the town. Know-Nothing Know-It-All: Most of the things she says after her verbal tic are either not true orTrivially Obvious.

characters.name: Toby
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: Cowardly Lion: Is scared of a lot of things, but can be a powerhouse when pressed.Being around Puss helps his confidence over the course of the series. Hero-Worshipper: For Puss, who he very much wants to be his best friend. The Squire: Eventually becomes Puss' squire and travels with him on adventures, though he sometimes causes more harm than good.

characters.name: The Sphinx
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: Half cat, half bird, this mythical beast once protected the Hourglass of Eurythion until Puss in Boots claimed it to save the orphans, and now she resides in San Lorenzo, guarding the entrance from anyone who can't answer her riddles... or happens to show up when she is napping or isn't paying attention. Defeat Means Friendship: After Puss takes the hourglass, she doesn't have anything else to do, so she comes to guard the entrance to San Lorenzo. Riddling Sphinx: She's a sphinx and she uses riddles to determine whoever is worthy of taking the Hourglass of Eurythion / entering San Lorenzo (though she proves much less competent at the latter task) Swiss-Cheese Security: She is often napping or away, or villains are just smart enough to answer her riddles. Valley Girl: Her accent and behavior are of this effect.

characters.name: El Guante Blanco
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: A famous cat adventurer who taught Puss in Boots everything he knows. His advanced age catching up with him, he initially decides to retire, but Puss is able to talk him out of it, making him the Brains of the Senior Puss Squad. When he wields the Goodsword, he can temporarily return to his prime. Affectionate Nickname: "Ginger" for Puss. Art Evolution: His "white glove" was just a light tip of white coloring at the end of his right paw, but in Season 3 onward, his entire paw is white. BFS: The Goodsword, relative to him. Since the sword gives it's userSuper-Strength, he can wield it easily. Cool Sword: His rapier is a gold and red version of Puss', implying Puss' rapier was a gift from Guante Blanco. The Goodsword as his replacement weapon also qualifies.

characters.name: Cleevil
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: Fantastic Racism: Faces this for being a goblin, and was apparently kicked out of several of her homes just because of it. Luckily, San Lorenzo gets past this and eventually accepts her like any other orphan child. Heel–Face Turn: Was originally aFall Guyfor the Duchess, but even so was aMinion with an F in Evilwho didn't realize how bad her boss was. She switched sides after being accepted by Puss and San Lorenzo. Hero-Worshipper: Becomes one for Puss very quickly. Even when she was still spying on him for the Duchess, her adoration for him was genuine. Afterwards, she seems to gain an interest in being a hero like him. I Just Want to Be Loved: Originally just wanted to be accepted, or to be able to make people accept her. Killed Off for Real:When her purpose as a Tulpa is completed Cleevil like Orange fades away, and, according to Sino, unlike Dulcinea she was not enough of a real person to continue existing after. It is unknown if this was undone when Puss and Dulcinea reversed time to before she ever met Puss.

characters.name: The Ninja Brothers
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: Toby's older brothers, Brandt, Dom, Chad, and Lamar. Big Brother Bully: They are this towards Toby and even kicked him out to the point he had to go to the orphanage. Unfortunately, this relationship does not change after Toby stands up to them as they continue to manipulate him.

characters.name: The Guild of Darkness
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: A guild comprised of three black cats, Phillip, Baltasar, and Francisco. The Aloner: Causing bad luck wherever they go tends to alienate them from others. Cloud Cuckoolander: Phillip is the quirkiest of the trio Deadpan Snarker: Baltasar, as theOnly Sane Man Flowery Elizabethan English: Francisco talks like this to such a degree, it can be hard to understand him.

characters.name: The Duchess
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: A soul-stealing witch who captures the souls of wizards to use in her spells. Artificial Limbs: Has a metal leg as a result of an encounter with Puss in Boots. Enemy Mine: While she hates Puss in Boots, when she learns of the return of the Bloodwolf, she agrees to join the Senior Puss Squad because the Bloodwolf is an enemy to all life. Joke Weapon: Her collection of souls is extremely thorough, even containing weird spells that even she isn't sure she needs, like a spell for conjuring sandbags. Ret-Gone:After upsetting her sisters, she was cursed by them to have all memory of her erased from existence.

characters.name: The Mole King
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: The King of theMole Kingdomthat lies deep below the magical earth underneath San Lorenzo, whose prized possession is a silver ball he nicknames "Bally" that shows his reflection. He is a dimwitted, childish boor with a short fuse, but is actually surprisingly fair and ends up one of the San Lorenzo's strongest - if most tumultuous - allies. Beware the Silly Ones: The one time him and Puss ever fought directly, the Mole King manhandled Puss handily offscreen. Cloud Cuckoo Lander: The Mole King cherishes his Bally because it shows him a show about great king like him, actually just his reflection. Hair-Trigger Temper: Prone to flying into a rage and trying to attack the town, albeit nearly always for a good reason. It's All About Me: The reason he nearly ruined his relationship with the Megamicre Queen. He expected her to follow him around doing whatever he wanted with zero attention or regard for her wants or desires. Dulcinea eventually teaches him to improve, and the two get married instead.

characters.name: Uli
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Pretends to be an adorable, silly but helpful little satyr. Turns out to be a villain out to summon a world-destroying monster. Evil Is Not a Toy: Uli learns this the hard way. He spends over three seasons trying to summon anAncient Evilknown as the Bloodwolf. Once summoned, the Bloodwolf wonders why he should be grateful toward Uli for any of this, before tossing him aside and telling him that he will be destroyed along with the world. Heel–Face Revolving Door: He initially pretends to be Puss' friend in order to trick him into summoning the Bloodwolf, and while waiting for his arrival, he decides to pretend to be good again in order to trick Puss into completing the ritual and then decides to atone and sacrifices himself. Manipulative Bastard: Trickseveryoneabout his true nature. The only person who ever suspected him was Puss at first, and even he is fooled eventually.

characters.name: Puss Dos
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: An imperfect clone of Puss in Boots he created in order for him to be in two places at once. The Cameo: Since his creation episode, he mostly appears randomly to deliver single lines of dialog before leaving, mostly as aCall-Backto the fact that he's still around. Cloudcuckoolander: His mind is not all there. Identical Twin ID Tag: Puss Dos wasn't created with a hat, so he wears one made out of paper instead. He is otherwise physically identical to Puss in Boots in every way besides voice and IQ. Two-Timer Date: Created as a means of dealing with this.

characters.name: Taranis
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: A boisterous and extraordinarily gullible thunder god who comes looking for a magic scepter in San Lorenzo, only to be chased out by the orphans. He later reappears, looking for Puss' help. Boisterous Bruiser: Speaks dramatically at all times, crowing pompously about his many powers. It's actually deserved, though he never uses his full powers against the orphans.]] The Ditz: He believes everything told him at face value, seems to have no conception of whether he's being insulted, and many of his dramatic proclamations do not actually make sense. It makes him easy to confuse.Cleevil (to the other orphans):This clown believes everything we tell him, right?Taranis (offscreen):You bet I do! Department of Redundancy Department: Rambles a lot, saying what is essentially the same thing over and over. The Dreaded: Puss becomes this to him after he hears enough of the orphans' outrageously exaggerated stories. Comes back to bite Puss later when Taranis shows up and expects him to be able to do all the things Taranis believes he can.

characters.name: The Eldritch Sisters
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: A trio of evil witch sisters -actually a quartet- who each were tasked with guarding a piece of the gem of the Crown of Souls. An eccentric and belligerent bunch, each sister puts Puss and friends though the wringer before letting go of their piece. Years before the seriesthere was a fourth Eldritch Sister, but an argument led to the others literally erasing her from all of their minds. In the present, the amnesiac Maldonna became The Duchess. Arc Villain: Collectively, for the first part of the fourth season. Big Bad Wannabe: Malviola is plenty powerful, but is fixated on being as much of a wicked with as possible due to her sisters teasing her her whole life. In the end, Dulcinea convinces her to be herself, and she decides to become the kind of witch who likes dancing and butterflies. De-power: Sort of. Maliflua uses special flutes to control animals. However, during their final fight Artephius steals her flute that could control Puss and Dulcinea, leaving her only with an entirely harmless flute that controls butterflies. It's implied she could acquire other flutes, but when last seen she has none of those.

characters.name: Great Mage Sino
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: A wise and powerful sorcerer who hundreds of years ago banished the Bloodwolf to the Netherworld and founded the City of San Lorenzo, filling it with magical artifacts which he used to create a barrier to shield the town from the rest of the world. Absent-Minded Professor: Though he's generally more serious and on the ball, it turns outArtephius' habit of comically forgetting to share important details and not remebering until way after it would've been useful comes from him. Berserk Button: If the voice coming from his statue in the first episode is taken to be his, he is not happy about the treasury being robbed, especially since the nature of the barrier protecting San Lorenzo means a citizen of San Lorenzo has to be partially at fault for this.Sino: Greed has broken the spell that concealed San Lorenzo from the world! Crazy-Prepared: After banishing the Bloodwolf, he made arrangements to allowThe Chosen Oneto banish him again, this time into a far more personalized pocket dimension, just in case he escaped. Godzilla Threshold: Despite hiding the Arcanum and himself away for the good of the world, he still put in contingency plans to reunite with the Arcanum, to only be used if it was absolutely necessary.

characters.name: The Tulpa
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: But Now I Must Go: After fulfilling their one task, the Tulpa in question fades into the beyond. I Am Who?: To better hide what they know, Green and White are unaware of their identities as Tulpa, but they can still awaken to their role when it is needed. Non-Standard Character Design: While Green and White can pass for normal people, their appearances do not quite line up with others of their apparent race. No-Sell: Because they are made from a part of Sino, they are immune to Sino's power. Odd Name Out: Orange is the only one who's color name doesn't match her primary color scheme.

characters.name: The Bloodwolf
characters.media_title: The Adventures of Puss in Boots
characters.description: A powerful entity who was sealed away in the Netherworld by the Great Mage Sino. Ancient Evil: He claims to be older than time itself. Animalistic Abomination: He's a demonic, wolf-like being that feeds on fear and claims to be older than time. Evil Is Bigger: One of the largest characters in the show. Greater-Scope Villain: To some extent, the entire show owes its existence to the Bloodwolf as San Lorenzo was built and veiled primarily to hide the Obelisk of Night to keep the Bloodwolf from ever escaping.
